_risk_level_doc: Medium: changes agent contracts across 4 markdown files; no infrastructure code changed; no security boundary affected. Incorrect spec could cause downstream dev cycles to silently omit provenance checks.

requirement.original: OOS item D — dev self-report provenance. The dev agent currently reports files_modified and files_created based on its own work-tree inspection at end of execution. In cycle dev-20260519-211515, dev claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were ALL already present in git HEAD from prior session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d). The fix has four parts: (1) commands/dev.md Step 8 captures baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot, (2) agents/dev.md mandates git-diff derivation for files_modified, (3) agents/dev.md adds observed_preexisting[], (4) agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md check provenance.
requirement.clarified: Establish a four-part markdown-only contract change that ensures dev.files_modified/files_created reflect only files actually changed in this cycle (via git diff vs a pre-dispatch baseline SHA), with clear handling for pre-existing dirty files, and provenance enforcement in QA and changelog-analyst.
requirement.what: Add baseline_head_sha + baseline_dirty_snapshot capture to commands/dev.md Step 8; mandate git-diff derivation in agents/dev.md Output Format; add observed_preexisting[] field; add provenance check to agents/qa.md; add anomaly classification to agents/changelog-analyst.md.
requirement.why: Dev agent self-reporting inflated file counts (pre-existing committed files) corrupts QA scope analysis, changelog-analyst enrichment scope, and commit attribution chain.

included: agents/qa.md provenance check (FAIL on inflated claims)
excluded: enforcement tooling beyond the four markdown files

success_criteria: commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template contains literal strings 'baseline_head_sha' and 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' in the dispatch payload body
agents/dev.md Output Format describes files_modified/files_created as git-diff-derived and includes observed_preexisting[] and baseline_head_sha fields
agents/qa.md contains a provenance check raising critical FAIL with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' when files_modified paths are absent from git diff and not in baseline_dirty_snapshot
agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2 classifies such paths as provenance_anomaly and excludes them from enrichment scope
Both QA and changelog-analyst gracefully skip when baseline_head_sha is absent (unborn repo edge case)
constraints: No hooks, no settings.json, no scripts — markdown only
Changes must be additive (no existing prose removed or weakened)
Diff command form must match agent stage: working-tree diff (git diff --name-only <sha>) for dev/QA pre-commit; committed diff (git diff --name-only <sha>..HEAD) for changelog-analyst post-commit
observed_preexisting[] is informational only; does not block QA or changelog-analyst

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Dev cycle dev-20260519-211515 claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were all pre-existing in git HEAD; QA and changelog-analyst accepted the list as ground truth.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: agents/dev.md Output Format does not mandate that files_modified/files_created be derived from a git diff vs a pre-dispatch baseline. No baseline SHA is captured at dispatch time. Dev agents perform work-tree inspection ('is this file in expected state?') rather than diffing, so files already in the correct state from prior commits are counted.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — this is a spec omission, not a regression from a specific commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The dev Output Format was designed for the common case where dev actually edits files; the pre-existing-state scenario was not anticipated.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: QA scope analysis, changelog-analyst enrichment, and commit attribution all consume dev.files_modified as ground truth. Inflated lists cause: (a) QA verifying files it shouldn't; (b) changelog-analyst deriving wrong commit type/scope; (c) audit trails showing false attribution.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Identified in cycle dev-20260519-211515; OOS item D in Codex prioritization report 2026-05-xx.
observations: agents/dev.md Output Format (lines 488-494) describes files_modified and files_created as non-null lists with no baseline SHA constraint — inferred from Read of the file
commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch prompt (lines 647-673) contains no baseline_head_sha or baseline_dirty_snapshot fields — inferred from Read of the file
agents/qa.md Step 5 Regression Testing (lines 396-430) does not include a provenance check against a baseline SHA — inferred from Read of the file
agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2 (lines 82-116) treats dev-report files as authoritative for enrichment with no anomaly classification — inferred from Read of the file
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — runtime observation of the dev cycle's actual false attribution is the user's report, not independently reproduced here)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User cited exact cycle ID (dev-20260519-211515) and specific prior-session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d). BA read all four target files and confirmed the absence of baseline-capture and diff-derivation language.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: agents/dev.md Output Format section (lines 488-494); commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template (lines 647-673)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not applicable — spec omission, not a regression from a specific commit

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: the orchestrator (commands/dev.md Step 8) captures baseline_head_sha (the HEAD SHA at dispatch time) and baseline_dirty_snapshot (list of untracked/modified files at dispatch) and passes both into the dev dispatch payload
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template includes 'baseline_head_sha' and 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' in the dispatch payload body (not only comments)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: agents/dev.md specifies that dev.files_modified must be computed from git diff --name-only $baseline_head_sha..HEAD (not work-tree inspection)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: agents/dev.md Output Format mandates git-diff derivation for files_modified/files_created; schema includes observed_preexisting[] field and baseline_head_sha top-level field in dev-report

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md add a check: if dev.files_modified lists a path not in git diff vs baseline, raise FAIL with primary_cause = 'dev_implementation'
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: agents/qa.md contains provenance check raising FAIL with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation'; agents/changelog-analyst.md classifies anomaly paths and excludes from enrichment

component_chain.evidence: Read of commands/dev.md lines 604-676 confirms Step 8 dispatch prompt template with no baseline fields

component_chain.role: Dev Output Format — defines dev-report schema including files_modified/files_created; insertion point for git-diff derivation rule, observed_preexisting[], and baseline_head_sha top-level field
component_chain.evidence: Read of agents/dev.md lines 488-574 confirms files_modified and files_created defined as non-null lists without baseline constraint

component_chain.role: QA Step 5 Regression Testing — insertion point for provenance check against baseline_head_sha
component_chain.evidence: Read of agents/qa.md lines 396-430 confirms no provenance check exists

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: **Top-level non-null lists** (CRITICAL): `dev.files_modified` and `dev.files_created` MUST be non-null lists at the `dev` root level
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Ensures dev-report is not missing file lists (required by commit.sh)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Extract `dev.files_modified[]` and `dev.files_created[]` arrays from the resolved path. These paths are used for commit message enrichment (deriving type/scope/summary). They do NOT add files to the commit that are not already in git status output.
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Clarifies enrichment-only role of dev-report in commit pipeline

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: dev claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were ALL already present in git HEAD from prior session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d)

measured.value: agents/dev.md Output Format defines files_modified and files_created as non-null lists with no baseline SHA or git-diff derivation constraint; commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch template has no baseline_head_sha field

expected.value: files_modified derived from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>; baseline_head_sha captured at dispatch and passed into dev payload; provenance check in QA and changelog-analyst
expected.source: User requirement verbatim + user requirement document docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260522-080646.md

evidence.gap: No baseline SHA is captured; no diff-derivation is mandated; no downstream provenance check exists — allowing inflated file lists to flow undetected through the quality chain

all_occurrences: commands/dev.md:647-673 (Step 8 dispatch template — no baseline fields)
agents/dev.md:488-494 (Output Format files_modified/files_created — no diff derivation)

reference_source.description: Four target markdown files read directly in this session
reference_source.url_or_location: agents/dev.md, commands/dev.md, agents/qa.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md (all read in session)

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt exists for this specific provenance issue

context.codebase_state: master branch; recent commits include 1a1d058 (Cycle 2 remediation), 50f2ca2 (style-inspector), 1bf636c (close-report ship), 6537d7d (hooks/commit fix), d23bfb6 (redev align)

development_approach.strategy: Four additive text insertions in existing markdown sections. All changes are new prose; no existing prose is removed or weakened. The key technical subtlety is the diff command form: working-tree diff (git diff --name-only <sha>) at dev/QA time vs committed diff (git diff --name-only <sha>..HEAD) at changelog-analyst time.

changes.what: Add a 'Pre-dispatch baseline capture' subsection immediately before the Dev dispatch prompt template. This subsection instructs the orchestrator to capture baseline_head_sha (git rev-parse HEAD) and baseline_dirty_snapshot (git status --porcelain) and include both as fields in the dev dispatch payload.
changes.before_description: Step 8 moves directly from test-writer gate evaluation to the dispatch prompt template with no baseline capture
changes.after_description: Step 8 includes a baseline capture block before dispatch; baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot appear as named fields in the dispatch payload
changes.rationale: Without the baseline capture, dev has no reference point to compute which files it actually changed vs which were already correct

changes.what: Replace the current description of files_modified/files_created ('non-null lists at the dev root level') with a requirement that they be derived from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' (working tree vs baseline). Add baseline_head_sha as a top-level dev-report field. Add observed_preexisting[] field with schema and description.
changes.before_description: files_modified and files_created are non-null lists; no derivation method is specified
changes.after_description: files_modified and files_created must be derived from git diff; observed_preexisting[] is a separate list for files confirmed correct but absent from the diff; baseline_head_sha is included as a top-level field
changes.rationale: Dev must report only what it actually changed; pre-existing-state confirmation is a separate informational category

changes.section: Step 5 Regression Testing (or new Step 5.5 Provenance Check)
changes.what: Add a provenance check subsection: read baseline_head_sha from context JSON or dev-report; compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working tree vs baseline); for every path in dev.files_modified absent from this diff AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise a critical FAIL finding with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'dev_provenance_violation'. Include graceful skip when baseline_head_sha is absent.
changes.before_description: Step 5 performs git diff HEAD~1 and dependency checks but no provenance check against baseline
changes.after_description: Step 5 includes provenance check that validates dev.files_modified against actual git diff from baseline_head_sha
changes.rationale: QA is the last line of defense before changelog-analyst and commit chain consume the file list

changes.what: After extracting files_modified and files_created from dev-report, add a provenance filter: compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD; classify any path in files_modified/files_created that is absent from this diff AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot as 'provenance_anomaly'; exclude provenance_anomaly paths from type/scope/summary enrichment derivation (stage them if in git status, but don't use them for commit message construction).
changes.before_description: Phase 2 uses dev-report files_modified/files_created as authoritative enrichment source
changes.after_description: Phase 2 applies provenance filter before using dev-report for enrichment; anomaly paths are still staged but excluded from commit message scope derivation
changes.rationale: Commit message attribution must reflect actual changes, not pre-existing files dev found in correct state

development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies by reading the four modified files and confirming: (1) baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot appear in commands/dev.md Step 8 dispatch payload; (2) agents/dev.md mandates git-diff derivation and has observed_preexisting[] and baseline_head_sha fields; (3) agents/qa.md provenance check exists with correct FAIL semantics; (4) agents/changelog-analyst.md has provenance_anomaly classification and enrichment exclusion rule.

_blast_radius_note: blast-radius-tool not applicable for markdown-only contract changes with no import/dependency graph

research_findings.reason: straightforward agent-contract fix with clear root cause and prescribed solution in the user requirement; no novel pattern or architecture choice requiring best-practices research

specialists_assessed.ui-specialist: SKIP — pure agent-prompt markdown change; no visual or UI component affected
specialists_assessed.architect: SKIP — contract change is within well-understood agent boundary; no new architectural pattern introduced
specialists_assessed.product-owner: SKIP — business logic is unambiguous: dev self-report must be git-diff-derived; no feature-completeness question
specialists_assessed.user: SKIP — requirement is fully specified by user verbatim in requirement document

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec was blocked by BA tool-policy (write target not in allowed_write_path_prefixes). Self-review substituted with 6 adversarial questions covering: (1) unborn-repo edge case for baseline_head_sha, (2) dirty-baseline handling for observed_preexisting[], (3) diff command form distinction between pre-commit (working-tree diff) and post-commit (..HEAD diff), (4) AC-1 verifiability strengthening, (5) changelog-analyst baseline_head_sha sourcing from dev-report top-level, (6) precise QA FAIL set definition excluding baseline_dirty_snapshot entries.

findings.issue: Unborn-repo edge case: if no commits exist, baseline_head_sha is null and git diff fails
findings.proposed_fix: Spec notes that when baseline_head_sha is absent/null, QA and changelog-analyst skip provenance check with warning (not FAIL). Added to AC5 and S1.
findings.rationale: Edge case is real; graceful degradation is the correct behavior

findings.issue: Dirty-baseline files (in baseline_dirty_snapshot) should not be flagged as provenance anomaly by QA
findings.proposed_fix: QA provenance check FAIL set = files_modified NOT IN git diff AND NOT IN baseline_dirty_snapshot. Added to AC3 and M4.
findings.rationale: Dev may legitimately commit files that were dirty at dispatch time; those should be observed_preexisting or legitimately modified, not anomalies

findings.issue: Diff command form: at dev/QA time changes are uncommitted so git diff --name-only <sha>..HEAD is empty
findings.proposed_fix: Use git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working-tree vs SHA) at dev/QA time; git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD at changelog-analyst time (post-commit). Added to Technical Hints and M4.
findings.rationale: Critical correctness issue — the spec must specify which diff form applies at each agent stage

findings.issue: baseline_head_sha must appear in dev-report top-level so changelog-analyst can read it without needing context JSON
findings.proposed_fix: agents/dev.md Output Format adds baseline_head_sha as a top-level dev-report field. Added to M2 and AC2.
findings.rationale: Changelog-analyst reads dev-report, not context JSON; it needs baseline_head_sha from that file

findings.issue: AC-1 verifiability: grep for string in comment does not prove it is in the dispatch payload
findings.proposed_fix: AC-1 strengthened to require the fields appear inside the dispatch payload block, not only in comments.
findings.rationale: Verifiability of AC-1 must be unambiguous

findings.issue: Two-repo scenario: main repo and nested repo have separate HEADs
findings.proposed_fix: Added to Edge Cases section; noted that baseline_head_sha_root and baseline_head_sha_nested may be needed, or documented as per-repo provenance check.
findings.rationale: The nested repo is a real second git repo; the spec should at least flag this

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 6 self-review findings incorporated: graceful skip on absent baseline, dirty-baseline exclusion from FAIL set, diff-command-form distinction, baseline_head_sha in dev-report top-level, AC-1 verifiability strengthened, two-repo edge case noted.
